bottles.name: Dragonette Seven
bottles.notes:
I didn't anticipate that our first bottle experience on Kazmi Cellars would be one where I wasn't actually drinking wine. But here we are.
It's the night before Mother's Day. I'm fresh off a hip replacement, on enough medication to sedate a large animal, and Rachel has been an absolute rock through all of it — taking care of me without complaint, without hesitation. The least I could do was open something special for her.
Dragonette is our oldest standing wine membership we still hold from Santa Barbara. It means something to us. So the 2017 Syrah was an easy choice — it's one of Rachel's favorites and honestly I don't think we could have done better.
This isn't your traditional big, brooding Syrah. Think medium-plus bodied, elegant more than powerful. We noticed plum, a little blueberry, a subtle spice that weaves through without ever taking over. Nothing heavy, nothing aggressive. Just a beautifully composed wine that's had just enough time in the bottle to find itself. The 2017 vintage was a perfect call.
It paired beautifully with lamb shank. Let it breathe for about 45 minutes to an hour beforehand. Trust me on that. The wine opens up and the pairing becomes something else entirely.
I did manage to steal one sip. It made me extremely jealous of Rachel. Worth every drop.
Who Should Grab This: If you love Syrah but find the big Napa style reds a little too much, Dragonette is your answer. Approachable, refined, and the kind of bottle you open when the moment deserves it.
The Kazmi Take: A membership we'll never give up. The 2017 is drinking beautifully right now. Don't wait too long on it. Happy Mother's Day, babe. 🍷
